steps.title: Interpret the function notation
steps.content:
In $h(x) = -16x^2 + 24x + 4$, the input $x$ is time in seconds and the output $h(x)$ is height in feet.
So $h(1.6) = 1.44$ means: at $x = 1.6$ seconds, the height equals $1.44$ feet.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "The height of the object 1.6 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is 1.44 feet.": Height at $1.6$ seconds is $1.44$ feet — matches the input/output interpretation.
  • "The height of the object 1.44 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is 1.6 feet.": Swaps the input and output (time $1.44$, height $1.6$). Eliminate.
  • "The height of the object 1.44 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is approximately 1.6 times as great as its initial height.": Talks about a ratio to initial height; the equation gives an absolute height, not a multiplicative comparison. Eliminate.
  • "The speed of the object 1.6 seconds after being thrown straight up in the air is approximately 1.44 feet per second.": Refers to speed, but $h(x)$ models height, not speed. Eliminate.
That matches the correct answer - A is correct.
